statement: No credentials, API keys, tokens, or secrets in any tracked file.
because: Tracked files are committed, replicated, mirrored, and cached. Once a secret enters git history, it is permanently exposed even after deletion — every clone, fork, and CI cache retains it. Secrets in source also bypass any audit trail provided by a secrets management system.
if_violated: Rotate the leaked credential immediately. Audit downstream system access during the exposure window. Rewrite git history to scrub the secret from all branches, tags, and forks. Public exposure cannot be undone — assume the secret is compromised forever.
instead: Read all secrets from environment variables loaded from a .env file that is in .gitignore, or from a managed secrets provider (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault, GCP Secret Manager, 1Password). Commit a .env.example with placeholder values. Use a pre-commit hook (gitleaks, trufflehog) to block accidental commits.
